title: Zoro Faces Fish-Man Strength
description: On pages 5–8, zoro faces fish-man strength focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as the Straw Hats crash into Arlong Park, and the fight against Arlong’s fish-men begins in earnest.
scene: On pages 5–8, zoro faces fish-man strength focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as the Straw Hats crash into Arlong Park, and the fight against Arlong’s fish-men begins in earnest.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Looks like I've chosen to follow a dumbass for my captain...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But it's still a 100 times better,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: than being in a pirate crew that would harm a lady.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: For a human, you're not half-bad but...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: A pirate going around spouting chivalrous lines? How half-assed...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Still not yet. My feet aren't budging one bit.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Would you like to test just how half-assed my chivalry really is, Mr. Fish?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Despite what you may think, I've spent half my life as a pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Don't understand the level that we fishermen are on.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Eh, you're only stretching it. My feet are still stuck as ever.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Is it still not coming out!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: You think you can just horse around,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Hurry up and get your feet out already!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why don't you play with me instead?

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
